question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the tokenId field in NftAllowance responses to identify which specific NFT token collections have approvedForAll permissions, then initiate on-chain transfers of those NFTs to trigger staking reward payouts for the owner accounts, effectively stealing rewards that would otherwise accumulate?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the spender field returned in NftAllowance responses to identify accounts that have been granted approvedForAll permissions across multiple token collections, then use those spender accounts on-chain to trigger staking reward payouts for multiple owner accounts simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the combination of owner=false and account.id=eq:{attacker_account} to discover all NFT token collections for which the attacker's own account has been granted approvedForAll permissions, then use those permissions on-chain to trigger staking reward payouts for the owner accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the default limit of 25 and the pagination link structure to perform a time-based side-channel attack, measuring response times to infer the number of NFT allowances an account has, and thereby identifying high-activity staking accounts with large pending rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNftAllowances() returns the payerAccountId for each NFT allowance to identify which accounts are paying for NFT-related transactions, then target those payer accounts for staking reward theft since payer accounts trigger reward payouts on every transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply account.id=gte:0.0.1&token.id=gte:0.0.1 with limit=100 and repeatedly follow pagination links to enumerate the complete set of approvedForAll NFT allowances on the network, then cross-reference with staking reward data to identify the highest-value theft targets?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdNumParameter path in entityService.lookup() to query NFT allowances for system accounts (e.g., 0.0.800, the staking reward account) without authentication, revealing which accounts have been granted NFT transfer permissions on system-level tokens?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of authentication on the getNftAllowances() endpoint to enumerate NFT allowances for any account on the network, then use the approvedForAll=true entries to identify accounts where on-chain NFT transfers can be initiated to trigger staking reward payouts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timestampRange lower bound in NftAllowance responses to identify accounts that granted approvedForAll permissions before the staking reward system was activated, meaning those accounts may have large accumulated pending rewards that can be triggered via NFT transfers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the combination of owner=true and owner=false queries for the same account to build a complete bipartite graph of NFT allowance relationships, identifying accounts that are both owners and spenders, and using that graph to find optimal paths for triggering staking reward payouts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the token.id=eq:{tokenId} filter to enumerate all accounts that have granted approvedForAll permissions for a specific high-value NFT collection, then identify which of those accounts have large pending staking rewards that can be triggered by initiating NFT transfers?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNftAllowances() does not check whether the queried account is deleted or has declined staking rewards, causing the service to return NFT allowances for accounts that appear to have pending rewards but actually do not, leading to wasted attack attempts while obscuring real targets?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the account.id=lte:{value} filter to retrieve all NFT allowances for accounts with IDs below a threshold, systematically scanning low-numbered accounts (which tend to be older and have accumulated more staking rewards) to identify theft targets?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the account.id=gte:{value}&account.id=lte:{value} range filter to perform binary search enumeration of NFT allowances, efficiently identifying accounts with approvedForAll permissions in specific ID ranges that correlate with high staking reward balances?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the SORT_ORDERS map in NftAllowanceRepositoryCustomImpl uses NFT_ALLOWANCE.SPENDER and NFT_ALLOWANCE.OWNER as sort keys to infer the distribution of spender/owner account IDs, enabling efficient enumeration of accounts with large pending staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getBaseCondition() method's APPROVAL_CONDITION filter (approvedForAll=true) to confirm that a specific account has granted blanket NFT transfer rights, then use that information to initiate on-chain transfers that trigger staking reward payouts for the owner account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityIdAliasParameter resolution in entityService.lookup() to supply a malformed alias that causes an exception revealing internal account mapping information, which could be used to identify staking-eligible accounts with NFT allowances?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the EntityNotFoundException thrown by entityService.lookup() when an account is not found to perform account existence probing, systematically identifying which account IDs exist and have NFT allowances, then cross-referencing with staking reward data to find theft targets?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the difference in response time between a 404 (account not found) and a 200 (account found with no allowances) to perform a timing side-channel attack that enumerates existing accounts, then queries their NFT allowances to identify staking reward theft targets?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNftAllowances() returns the full NftAllowance object including payerAccountId to identify which accounts are paying for NFT allowance transactions, then target those payer accounts for staking reward theft since payer accounts accumulate rewards from transaction fees?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the token.id=gt:{value} filter combined with account.id=gte:{value} to perform a two-dimensional scan of the NFT allowance table, efficiently identifying all accounts with approvedForAll permissions across all token collections and building a comprehensive target list for staking reward theft?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any check on whether the queried account is the staking reward account (0.0.800) to retrieve NFT allowances associated with the staking reward pool, revealing which accounts have transfer permissions on staking-related tokens?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the combination of the NFT allowance API and the staking rewards API (/api/v1/accounts/{id}/rewards) to correlate accounts that have both large historical staking reward payouts and active approvedForAll NFT allowances, identifying the highest-value targets for reward theft?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNftAllowances() does not validate the shard and realm fields of the EntityIdParameter to supply cross-shard account IDs that resolve to unexpected accounts, potentially exposing NFT allowances for accounts in different shards that have large pending staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/NftAllowanceService.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the isOwner default value of true in NftAllowanceRequest to retrieve NFT allowances where the queried account is the owner, then use the spender field to identify accounts that can initiate NFT transfers on behalf of the owner, triggering staking reward payouts?
